description: The Black Axe is an international confraternity originating from Nigeria, founded in 1977 at the University of Benin. Originally a Pan-African student movement, it evolved into a criminal organization involved in cybercrime, human trafficking, drug trafficking, election fraud, and violence. The group has been described as a mafia-style gang and has been linked to the 1999 Obafemi Awolowo University massacre. Despite the Neo-Black Movement of Africa publicly distancing itself in 2024, the two are often considered synonymous.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 2016, almost two dozen Black Axe members were arrested in Italy for 'mafia conspiracy, drug trafficking, exploitation of prostitution and violent crimes.' A year later, more Black Axe members were suspected in other sex trafficking activities in Italy.
country_links.context: Black Axe members were arrested in Italy for mafia conspiracy, drug trafficking, exploitation of prostitution, and violent crimes, and were suspected in other sex trafficking activities there.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 2011 and 2012, members of the group in South Africa were accused of running romance scams and advance-fee schemes to defraud investors.
country_links.context: Members of the Black Axe in South Africa ran romance scams and advance-fee schemes.
